recalls.defect: The front passenger air bag inflator may rupture due to propellant degradation after long-term exposure to high humidity and temperature cycling.
recalls.consequence: An inflator rupture may result in metal fragments striking occupants, increasing the risk of serious injury or death.
recalls.action: Dealers will replace the front passenger air bag inflator, free of charge.

recalls.defect: The door latch assembly may corrode, allowing the door to unlatch while driving.
recalls.consequence: A door that opens while driving increases the risk of occupant ejection and injury.
recalls.action: Dealers will replace the door latch assemblies, free of charge.

recalls.defect: Engine wiring harness connectors may loosen over time, causing intermittent stalling.
recalls.consequence: An engine stall while driving increases the risk of a crash.
recalls.action: Dealers will inspect and secure the wiring harness connectors, free of charge.

recalls.defect: The brake master cylinder may leak fluid internally, reducing braking performance.
recalls.consequence: Reduced braking performance lengthens stopping distance, increasing crash risk.
recalls.action: Dealers will replace the brake master cylinder, free of charge.

complaintSamples.narrative: While driving at highway speed the dash lights flickered and the engine lost power. The dealer replaced a wiring connector under recall.
The air bag warning light stays on. The dealer confirmed the passenger inflator is part of an open recall and ordered parts.

investigations.subject: Loss of motive power
investigations.summary: NHTSA opened a preliminary evaluation into reports of intermittent stalling on 2015-2016 model year vehicles.
